closure.def: 關閉、終止、結案的人物 (單數)
closure.ex: The sudden factory closure caused many local people jobs.

clot.def: (血液等)凝塊、笨蛋、使凝結的人的人
clot.ex: He was rushed to hospital due to a serious blood clot.

cloth.def: 布、織物、桌布、僧侶(總稱)的人的人物 (單數)
cloth.col: piece of cloth, table cloth, man of the cloth
cloth.ex: She used a soft piece of damp cloth to clean the glass.

clothe.def: 穿衣、覆蓋、授權的人的人
clothe.ex: They worked very hard to feed and clothe their children.

clothes.col: clean clothes, school clothes, casual clothes
clothes.ex: She always wears smart and expensive clothes to work.

clotheshorse.def: 晾衣架、愛穿衣服的人的人物 (單數)
clotheshorse.ex: She hung the wet laundry on the wooden clotheshorse.

clothesline.ex: The heavy clothesline was full of bright summer shirts.

clothespin/clothespeg.ex: She used a plastic clothespin to secure her dress.

clothier.def: 成衣商、布商的人物 (單數)
clothier.ex: The fine clothier had a wide selection of silk ties.

clothing.def: 服裝(總稱)、覆蓋物的人 (或物) (服)
clothing.ex: They recommended wearing warm protective clothing.

clotting.def: 凝結、形成凝塊的人 (或物)
clotting.ex: Vitamin K is essential for normal blood clotting.

cloud.def: 雲、陰影、使模糊、憂慮的人的人物
cloud.col: cloud over, on cloud nine (極樂), rain cloud
cloud.ex: A large and dark rain cloud appeared in the west tonight.

cloudburst.ex: A sudden cloudburst drenched the city within minutes.

clouded.def: 陰雲密佈的、模糊的人的人 (或物)
clouded.ex: His clouded vision made it very difficult to drive tonight.

cloudiness.ex: The weather report predicted increased cloudiness tonight.

cloudless.ex: The morning was bright with a perfect cloudless sky.

cloudy.def: 多雲的、陰天的、渾濁的人人物 (雲)
cloudy.ex: It was a cold and cloudy day, so we stayed at home.

clout.def: 敲擊、權力、影響力的人物 (單數)
clout.ex: The big company has a lot of political clout in the city.

clove.ex: The recipe calls for a small fresh clove of garlic.

cloven.def: 劈開了的、分裂的人的人 (或物)
cloven.ex: The devil is often described as having a cloven hoof.

clover.def: 三葉草、苜蓿的人物 (單數)
clover.col: four-leaf clover (幸運), in clover (生活優渥)
clover.ex: She was lucky enough to find a four-leaf clover today.

cloverleaf.def: 三葉草形、(陸)立體交叉道人物 (單數)
cloverleaf.ex: The highway cloverleaf made the junction very efficient.

clown.def: 小丑、笨蛋、扮小丑的人的人
clown.ex: The circus clown made all the young children laugh.

clowning.ex: The boys were clowning around in the schoolyard today.

clownish.def: 滑稽的、小丑般的人的人物
clownish.ex: His clownish behavior was not suitable for a meeting.

cloy.def: 倒胃口、使生膩、厭煩的人的人
cloy.ex: Too much very sweet food can cloy the appetite quickly.

cloying.def: 甜得發膩的、令人厭煩的人的人物
cloying.ex: The perfume had a cloying sweetness that I disliked.

club.def: 俱樂部、棍棒、(撲)梅花、參與的人物 (單數)
club.col: golf club, night club, join a club, club together (合資)
club.ex: They decided to club together to buy a wedding gift.

clubhouse.ex: We met in the grand golf clubhouse for a drink today.

cluck.def: (母雞)咯咯叫、發咯咯聲的人物 (單數)
cluck.ex: The old mother hen gave a loud cluck to her young chicks.

clucking.def: 正在咯咯叫的的的人 (或物)
clucking.ex: The clucking hens were searching for food in the yard.

clue.def: 線索、跡象、提供線索的人的人
clue.col: clue in (提供資訊), get a clue, cryptic clue
clue.ex: The police are searching for more clues at the bank.

clueless.ex: He was completely clueless about how the machine works.

clump.def: 叢、簇、沉重的腳步聲的人的人
clump.col: clump of trees, clump of grass
clump.ex: There was a dense clump of trees at the end of the road.

clumping.def: 叢生、沉重走動的人 (或物)
clumping.ex: I heard him clumping up the wooden stairs in boots.

clumsily.ex: He moved clumsily and knocked over the water glass.

clumsiness.ex: The mistake was caused by his natural clumsiness.

clumsy.def: 笨拙的、不優雅的人人物 (笨)
clumsy.ex: That was a very clumsy attempt to fix the mirror today.

clung.def: (cling的過去式及過去分詞)的人的人 (或物)
clung.ex: The baby clung tight to her mother's hand today.

cluster.col: star cluster, cluster around, cluster headache
cluster.ex: The students began to cluster around the teacher's desk.

clutch.def: 緊握、離合器、一批、抓的人的人
clutch.col: clutch at, clutch bag, automatic clutch
clutch.ex: She managed to clutch her purse tight in the crowd tonight.

clutching.ex: By making these excuses, he is just clutching at straws.

clutter.def: 雜亂、凌亂、使混亂的人的人
clutter.ex: Please don't clutter up the room with all your boxes.

cluttered.def: 凌亂的、混雜的人的人 (或物)
cluttered.ex: His cluttered desk was full of old papers and books tonight.
